name: Fixtures self-test closure + --target routing (NDEBT-009, NDEBT-015, NDEBT-016)

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Phase-006 F-052: close the tools/fixtures/ dormancy. Three coupled parts. (NDEBT-009) A standing self-test tools/fixtures_self_test.sh + a fixtures_self_test CI job run EVERY shipped fixture through its targeted surface and assert the SPECIFIC verdict -- validate.sh --target for check-level fixtures, the verify_lib.sh primitives for primitive-level ones (incl. two git-scratch primitives), and a tools/skill.json substitution for the C13 negative fixture -- with a completeness guard so no fixture can silently go dormant. (NDEBT-015) check_c11_or_c4_target's router recognizes the three ecosystem families (verdict+execution_id -> preflight_verdict; the six *_references arrays -> ecosystem_baseline; closure_criteria -> engineering_finding), dispatching to a new check_c12_target that emits a discriminating [C12] verdict; before this, --target on any ecosystem fixture misrouted to C4/C11 and failed regardless of polarity. (NDEBT-016) C12's polarity classifier is case-insensitive with a naming-conformance guard that FAILs a basename carrying a neg/invalid look-alike that is not the canonical delimited-lowercase token.
design_notes.non_vacuity_thesis: The self-test's whole purpose is to catch a check gone vacuous, so it must not itself be vacuous. The trap discovered empirically: most .md fixtures already exit non-zero under --target from incidental C1/C2 frontmatter failures unrelated to what they test (stale_payload_pass.md exits 1 but its targeted check C10 correctly PASSES), so an exit-code-only self-test would pass on the WRONG check. The harness therefore asserts the specific [C<N>] verdict line (or a verify_lib primitive return), never a bare exit code. Proven load-bearing three ways (E03/E04/E10): making a negative's targeted check pass, adding an unaccounted fixture, and removing a listed fixture each fail the self-test.
design_notes.f051_drift_corrected_in_scope: DISCOVERED during F-052 and corrected in-scope (NDEBT-005-class enumeration-completeness miss, F-027/F-049 precedent for adjacent-drift correction, disclosed here + in the PR + CHANGELOG Fixed): feature 051 made methodology/+ecosystem/ injected/required and narrowed the payload skip set to registry/+docs/ in the CODE (skipped_dirs, SKIPPED_DIR_PREFIXES, the C9 allow-list) and the code-adjacent comment, but left four LIVE-STATE self-descriptions stale -- the C4 --help, the C13 --help, the C13 function doc comment, and the C13 payload-mode PASS echo all still named methodology/+ecosystem/ among the skipped dirs and said the carve-out was 'pending the F-051 payload-contract decision'. Shipping consumer-facing --help that states the opposite of the truth is exactly the narrative drift the framework exists to prevent, so all four were corrected to name only registry/+docs/. Historical change_log/CHANGELOG entries that were true when written are left intact (AH-3).
design_notes.scope_overage_disclosure: SCOPE FLAG (large): the deliverable is 678 lines changed (git numstat ins+del at 86a67ab: validate.sh 224+67, fixtures_self_test.sh 297 new, README 25+5, compliance.yml 22, CHANGELOG 30, DEBT 4+4) vs the 180-line plan-of-record estimate (feature_list_006.json) -- ~3.8x, which EXCEEDS 3x the recent rolling average (049=164, 050=194, 051=155; avg 171, 3x=513; 678 > 513). The overage is inherent to the acceptance tests, not scope creep: acceptance test 1 mandates 'every shipped negative fixture', so the harness must cover all 41 fixtures (29 --target rows + 5 verify_lib primitives incl. 2 git-scratch + C13 substitution + a completeness guard) and is ~297 lines alone; the validate.sh routing/conformance/help work is another ~291. The planning estimate was simply low for a comprehensive, non-vacuous, complete-coverage self-test. Cumulative 513+678 = 1191/2236 ceiling (53%), well under the 130% halt. Surfaced to the operator for acknowledgment; the PR is held for operator merge and the operator may redirect at review.
design_notes.process_honesty: Single-session execution, roles collapsed (046-051 precedent, disclosed at phase activation): contract, implementation, verification, and QA authored and executed in-session; all 12 entries ran live with captured evidence (fail-fast commands, NDEBT-019 lesson); adversarial probes (E03/E04/E07/E10) use MINIMAL scratch copies of the tree (tools/+schema/+NIZAM.json) under a session-scratch root, never mutating the real repository, per the NDEBT-013/020 probe-isolation rule; the C13 substitution inside the harness backs up and restores tools/skill.json with an EXIT-trap backstop.

files_create.purpose: NDEBT-009: standing self-test harness. Runs every tools/fixtures/ fixture through its targeted surface (validate.sh --target / verify_lib primitives / C13 substitution), asserts the specific verdict (never a bare exit code), and a completeness guard fails on any unaccounted fixture. Framework-internal; run as its own CI job.

files_modify.purpose: NDEBT-015: check_c11_or_c4_target's router recognizes the three ecosystem families and dispatches to a new check_c12_target (single-file schema validation emitting [C12]). NDEBT-016: C12's polarity classifier is case-insensitive with a naming-conformance guard rejecting non-canonical neg/invalid look-alikes. Help text (--target .json routing, C12, C13) synced. F-051-drift correction: the C4/C13 --help, the C13 function comment, and the C13 payload PASS echo now name only registry/+docs/ as skipped (methodology/+ecosystem/ are injected/required since F-051).
Add the fixtures_self_test CI job (checkout + setup-python + pip install jsonschema pyyaml + bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh) alongside validate and e2e_bootstrap.

0.5.0: document tools/fixtures_self_test.sh (new subsection), the C12 --target ecosystem routing (NDEBT-015) and naming-conformance guard (NDEBT-016) in the C12 row, and correct the C13 row's payload carve-out to registry/+docs/ only (F-051 made methodology/+ecosystem/ injected).
[Unreleased] Added: the fixtures self-test + CI job (NDEBT-009), C12 --target routing (NDEBT-015), C12 conformance guard (NDEBT-016). Fixed: the F-051 self-description drift.
0.22.0: NDEBT-009, NDEBT-015, NDEBT-016 -> Resolved with resolution notes.

non_goals: No change to what the default/--payload sweeps enforce on the framework's own tree (default stays 13/13, payload 11/11); C12's full-sweep polarity/dormancy guard is unchanged except for the added case-insensitive conformance gate.

No new SHIPPED fixtures: the NDEBT-016 dodge shapes and the NDEBT-009 adversarial cases are exercised in scratch copies during verification, never committed (a shipped dodge fixture would itself fail conformance / go dormant).

The self-test is framework-internal (reads tools/fixtures/, a dev/QA concern) and is NOT added to the bootstrap payload contract or NIZAM.json index; it mirrors tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh's precedent (a tools/ .sh not in the shipped-doc set).
No edit to any .agent/evidence/* historical capture, and no change to the verify_lib.sh primitives themselves (they are exercised, not modified).
The broader F-051 leftover audit beyond the four live-state validator self-descriptions is not performed here; historical change_log/CHANGELOG entries true when written are left intact per AH-3.

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD~1 = 37185da, the F-051 merge / PR #30 squash): before this deliverable there was NO tools/fixtures_self_test.sh, the router had NO ecosystem routes, C12 had NO conformance guard, and compliance.yml had NO fixtures_self_test job.
verification.command:
set -e
git cat-file -e HEAD~1:tools/fixtures_self_test.sh 2>/dev/null && { echo 'VACUITY: harness already present at HEAD~1'; exit 1; } || true
git show HEAD~1:tools/validate.sh | grep -Fq 'ecosystem:preflight_verdict' && { echo 'VACUITY: routing already present'; exit 1; } || true
git show HEAD~1:tools/validate.sh | grep -Fq 'LOOKALIKE = re.compile' && { echo 'VACUITY: conformance already present'; exit 1; } || true
git show HEAD~1:.github/workflows/compliance.yml | grep -Fq 'fixtures_self_test' && { echo 'VACUITY: CI job already present'; exit 1; } || true
echo 'E01 PASS: harness/routing/conformance/CI-job all absent at HEAD~1 (anti-vacuity)'

verification.acceptance_test: SELF-TEST GREEN + EXIT ASSERTED: tools/fixtures_self_test.sh exits 0 and reports every fixture accounted for with zero failures.
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1; test $rc -eq 0 && printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Eq 'SELF-TEST OK: [0-9]+/[0-9]+ fixtures accounted for, 0 failed' && echo "E02 PASS (rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: NON-VACUITY (headline): when a negative fixture's TARGETED check is made to PASS, the self-test FAILs -- so it genuinely catches a check gone vacuous, not merely a non-zero exit. Probed in a minimal scratch copy (never the real tree).
verification.command:
set -e
S="$SB/e03"; rm -rf "$S"; mkdir -p "$S"; cp -a tools schema NIZAM.json "$S/"
printf '# resolvable now\n\nThis body cites tools/verify_lib.sh which resolves fine.\n' > "$S/tools/fixtures/unresolved_path.md"
set +e; out=$(bash "$S/tools/fixtures_self_test.sh" 2>&1); rc=$?; set -e
test $rc -ne 0
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq 'FAIL target +unresolved_path.md'
rm -rf "$S"
echo "E03 PASS: self-test FAILs when a negative fixture's targeted check goes vacuous (rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: COMPLETENESS GUARD is load-bearing BOTH directions: an unaccounted fixture on disk FAILs, and a manifest row naming an absent fixture FAILs. Probed in a minimal scratch copy.
verification.command:
set -e
S="$SB/e04"; rm -rf "$S"; mkdir -p "$S"; cp -a tools schema NIZAM.json "$S/"
cp "$S/tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_valid.json" "$S/tools/fixtures/zzz_new_dormant.json"
set +e; a=$(bash "$S/tools/fixtures_self_test.sh" 2>&1); arc=$?; set -e
test $arc -ne 0; printf '%s\n' "$a" | grep -Fq 'zzz_new_dormant.json'
rm -f "$S/tools/fixtures/zzz_new_dormant.json"
mv "$S/tools/fixtures/exempt_paths.md" "$S/exempt_paths.md.bak"
set +e; b=$(bash "$S/tools/fixtures_self_test.sh" 2>&1); brc=$?; set -e
test $brc -ne 0; printf '%s\n' "$b" | grep -Fq 'not on disk'
rm -rf "$S"
echo "E04 PASS: completeness guard fails on unaccounted (rc=$arc) and on missing (rc=$brc)"

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-015: --target against each of the three ecosystem families routes to [C12] with DISCRIMINATING polarity -- valid PASSes, negative FAILs (acceptance test 2).
verification.command:
set -e
for f in ecosystem_baseline_valid preflight_verdict_pass engineering_finding_valid; do
  bash tools/validate.sh --target "tools/fixtures/$f.json" 2>&1 | grep -Eq '^\[C12\] PASS'
done
for f in ecosystem_baseline_neg_missing_revision preflight_verdict_invalid_verdict engineering_finding_neg_missing_owner; do
  set +e; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "tools/fixtures/$f.json" 2>&1); rc=$?; set -e
  test $rc -ne 0
  printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq '^\[C12\] FAIL'
done
echo 'E05 PASS: all 3 ecosystem families route to [C12] with discriminating polarity'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-015 ANTI-REGRESSION: the pre-existing routes are unchanged -- invalid_qa_verdict->C11, invalid_contract->C11, invalid_run_state->C11, broken_index->C4, NIZAM.json->C4 PASS. The new ecosystem routes did not steal any existing route.
verification.command:
set -e
bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/invalid_qa_verdict.json 2>&1 | grep -Eq '^\[C11\] FAIL'
bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/invalid_contract.json 2>&1 | grep -Eq '^\[C11\] FAIL'
bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/invalid_run_state.json 2>&1 | grep -Eq '^\[C11\] FAIL'
bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/broken_index.json 2>&1 | grep -Eq '^\[C4\] FAIL'
bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json 2>&1 | grep -Eq '^\[C4\] PASS'
echo 'E06 PASS: qa_verdict/contract/run_state/C4 routes unchanged'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-016: all three dodge shapes are rejected -- uppercase (_NEG_) and full-word (_negative_) by the naming-conformance guard, and the false-trigger (a schema-valid doc under a reserved _invalid_ token) by the polarity assertion (acceptance test 3). Probed in a minimal scratch copy.
verification.command:
set -e
S="$SB/e07"; rm -rf "$S"; mkdir -p "$S"; cp -a tools schema NIZAM.json "$S/"
V="$S/tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_valid.json"
cp "$V" "$S/tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_NEG_upper.json"
cp "$V" "$S/tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_negative_word.json"
cp "$V" "$S/tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_invalid_masquerade.json"
set +e; out=$(cd "$S" && bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); set -e
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Fq 'ecosystem_baseline_NEG_upper.json: non-conforming'
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Fq 'ecosystem_baseline_negative_word.json: non-conforming'
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Fq 'ecosystem_baseline_invalid_masquerade.json: negative fixture unexpectedly VALIDATED'
rm -rf "$S"
echo 'E07 PASS: uppercase+full-word rejected by conformance; false-trigger rejected by polarity assertion'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-016 does not disturb the shipped set: the hardened, case-insensitive classifier + conformance gate keeps every shipped fixture conforming, so the default sweep C12 stays PASS and SUMMARY stays 13/13.
verification.command:
set -e
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1)
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq '^\[C12\] PASS'
printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY: 13 passed, 0 failed'
echo 'E08 PASS: hardened C12 keeps the shipped fixtures green (default 13/13)'

verification.acceptance_test: C13 NEGATIVE FIXTURE EXERCISED: the self-test substitutes skill_index_neg_dangling_module.json for tools/skill.json, observes [C13] FAIL, and RESTORES skill.json (working tree clean afterward) -- closing the standing-wiring F-049 deferred to F-052.
verification.command:
set -e
out=$(bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh 2>&1)
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Fq 'c13-substitute skill_index_neg_dangling_module.json -> [C13] FAIL'
git diff --quiet -- tools/skill.json
echo 'E09 PASS: C13 negative fixture exercised via substitution; skill.json restored clean'

verification.acceptance_test: PRIMITIVE FIXTURES EXERCISED + load-bearing: the self-test drives all five verify_lib primitives (incl. the two git-scratch ones), and a corrupted section_grep_fail fixture makes the self-test FAIL. Probed in a minimal scratch copy.
verification.command:
set -e
out=$(bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh 2>&1)
for p in vlib_no_stale_payload vlib_section_grep vlib_path_resolves vlib_version_increased vlib_scope_guard; do
  printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq "OK +primitive +$p"
done
S="$SB/e10"; rm -rf "$S"; mkdir -p "$S"; cp -a tools schema NIZAM.json "$S/"
printf '# x\n\n## Target Section\n\nSECTION_GREP_MARKER planted in the target span.\n' > "$S/tools/fixtures/section_grep_fail.md"
set +e; b=$(bash "$S/tools/fixtures_self_test.sh" 2>&1); brc=$?; set -e
test $brc -ne 0; printf '%s\n' "$b" | grep -Eq 'FAIL .*vlib_section_grep fail'
rm -rf "$S"
echo 'E10 PASS: all 5 primitives exercised; primitive assertions load-bearing'

verification.acceptance_test: CI WIRING + REGRESSION BUNDLE: compliance.yml carries the fixtures_self_test job (with pip install jsonschema pyyaml) running the harness; default 13/13, payload 11/11, and e2e all still green.
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq 'fixtures_self_test:' .github/workflows/compliance.yml
grep -Fq 'bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh' .github/workflows/compliance.yml
awk '/fixtures_self_test:/{f=1} f&&/pip install jsonschema pyyaml/{print "pip-ok"; exit}' .github/workflows/compliance.yml | grep -Fq pip-ok
bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY: 13 passed, 0 failed'
bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1 | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 11 passed, 0 failed'
bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh >/dev/null 2>&1
echo 'E11 PASS: CI job wired (jsonschema/pyyaml); default 13/13, payload 11/11, e2e green'

verification.acceptance_test: DELIVERABLE CONFINEMENT + DOCS/DEBT: HEAD~1..HEAD touches exactly the six contracted files and no .agent path; DEBT 0.22.0 has NDEBT-009/015/016 in Resolved; README 0.5.0; CHANGELOG names the harness.
verification.command:
set -e
test "$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1..HEAD | LC_ALL=C sort | tr '\n' ' ')" = '.github/workflows/compliance.yml CHANGELOG.md docs/planning/DEBT.md tools/README.md tools/fixtures_self_test.sh tools/validate.sh '
test -z "$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1..HEAD -- .agent/)"
grep -Fq 'version: 0.22.0' docs/planning/DEBT.md
python3 - <<'PY'
import re
sect=None; resolved=set()
for l in open('docs/planning/DEBT.md'):
    if l.startswith('## Open'): sect='open'
    elif l.startswith('## Resolved'): sect='resolved'
    m=re.match(r'\| (NDEBT-\d+) ', l)
    if m and sect=='resolved': resolved.add(m.group(1))
    if l.startswith('| NDEBT-009 ') or l.startswith('| NDEBT-015 ') or l.startswith('| NDEBT-016 '):
        assert sect=='resolved', l[:14]+' not Resolved'
for n in ('NDEBT-009','NDEBT-015','NDEBT-016'): assert n in resolved, n
print('debt ok')
PY
grep -Fq 'version: 0.5.0' tools/README.md
sed -n '/## \[Unreleased\]/,/## \[0.7.0\]/p' CHANGELOG.md | grep -Fq 'fixtures_self_test'
echo 'E12 PASS: confined to the 6 files, zero .agent; DEBT 0.22.0 (009/015/016 Resolved); README 0.5.0; CHANGELOG synced'

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured output is in .agent/evidence/052-verify-NN.txt (12 entries), plus the consolidated .agent/evidence/052/verification.txt (12 EXIT:0 markers) and .agent/evidence/052/diff.patch (the deliverable diff). Commands run from the repo root at deliverable commit (HEAD), with HEAD~1 = the F-051 merge 37185da as the anti-vacuity anchor; adversarial probes (E03/E04/E07/E10) build MINIMAL scratch copies (tools/+schema/+NIZAM.json) under a session-scratch root $SB, never mutating the real tree.

approvals.process_note: Operator-directed phase execution (ungated DAG root, operator chose 052 via AskUserQuestion after PR #30 merged). No separate Loop-1 gate agents; the 12-entry suite executed live with captured evidence; QA record .agent/qa/052.json documents the independent full re-run. LARGE SCOPE OVERAGE disclosed (see design_notes.scope_overage_disclosure): 678 lines vs 180 estimated (~3.8x), exceeding 3x the rolling average (513) -- inherent to the 'every fixture' acceptance test, not scope creep; the PR is held for operator acknowledgment/ratification.
